root_motivation: The four Gen-9/10 investigation crates are disconnected CLIs; without an orchestrator a human must manually pipe WatchEvent→hypotheses→probes→findings, making autonomous investigation impossible.
user_persona: AtScale platform operator running automated anomaly investigation

unfakeable_metric.name: tests_pass_count
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --manifest-path /Users/jsy/Documents/projects/mcp-investigation-orchestrator/Cargo.toml 2>&1 | grep 'test result' | awk '{print $6}' | tr -d ';'

acceptance_criteria.description: Happy path: given a fixture WatchEvent and describe_model, the orchestrator produces at least one Finding with status Confirmed or Refuted.
Budget halt: with max_queries=1 and at least two hypotheses, exactly one probe runs and output notes the budget halt.
Idempotent upsert: running the same WatchEvent twice against the same store increments recurrence_count without duplicating the finding_id.

scope: Rust CLI binary that handles one WatchEvent per invocation
Builds a ConceptGraph from describe_model JSON and calls mcp-hypothesis-engine run_engine
Calls BudgetLedger::check() before each probe subprocess call
Invokes mcp-probe-executor as a subprocess with temporary hypothesis and budget JSON files
Persists the resolved investigation through FindingStore::record()
Emits a JSON array of Finding records to stdout

five_whys_trace.a: No cross-crate wiring task existed until now.
